name: Couscous Salad W/Saffron & Currants
recipeIngredient: 3 cups Vegetable Stock, Homemade, 750 mL, 24 fl oz,
1 tbsp Fresh Cilantro or Parsley, chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. See recipe for making vegetable stock or chicken stock, homemade.
2. NOTE: Original recipe called for 1/3 cup olive oil (155 mL / 5 fl oz).
3. Couscous is a tiny pasta that has long been popular in North Africa and is
so quick and easy to make. You can add ingredients to this list or omit
items if you don't care for them; this is such an adaptable salad or side
dish.
4. In a medium saucepan over high heat, bring the stock and saffron to a boil
and season with salt and pepper.
5. Place the couscous in a large nonreactive bowl and pour the boiling stock
over it. Cover and set aside for 1-2 minutes, or until all the liquid has
been absorbed.
6. Fluff the couscous up with a fork and set it aside.
7. In a medium bowl, mix together the onion, garlic, tomato, ginger, currants,
cilantro, and lime juice. Slowly whisk in the olive oil and season with
salt and pepper.
8. Pour the mixture over the couscous and mix well.
9. Serve the Couscous Salad at room temperature.
